summary: Broad fighter decompilation/matching work across Crazy Hand, Kirby, Popo/Nana, and Ice Climbers item headers. The PR replaced many placeholder `/// #...` stubs with matching C implementations, corrected several fighter attribute field types, added missing item/function prototypes, and used stack-padding/inlining/data-layout tactics to improve matching. decomp.dev reported +7136 matched bytes, 20 new matches, and a GALE01 matched-code increase to 60.76% (+0.18%). There was no PR body and no human review discussion in the slice.
what_changed: Implemented several Crazy Hand routines in `ftCh_Init.c`, including `ftCh_Init_80156198`, `ftCh_Init_80156310`, `ftCh_Wait1_0_Anim`, `ftCh_Init_80156AD8`, `fn_801588B8`, and `fn_8015B548`.
Crazy Hand wait AI/control logic now uses a typed local/global table `struct ftCh_Init_803D4878_t` with state indices, `u8_pair` ranges, and selection bytes instead of an anonymous flat byte array.
Crazy Hand attribute fields in `types.h` were corrected from `s32` to `float` for positions/offsets such as `x20`, `x6C`, `x70`, `x84`, `x90`, `x94`, `xA0`, `xB0`, and `xB4`.

Crazy Hand bomb spawning logic in `fn_801588B8` was implemented using joint positions from `FtPart_RLegJA`, `FtPart_BustN`, `FtPart_LHandN`, and `FtPart_L3rdNa`, then spawning `It_Kind_CrazyHand_Bomb` via `it_802F0F6C`.
Kirby init work implemented `ftKb_UnkIntBoolFunc0` for hat part/dobj swapping and `ftKb_SpecialN_800F1BAC` for changing copied-special hat kind, callbacks, SFX, and death callbacks.

Kirby headers were corrected so several `ftKb_SpecialNYs_801092DC`-style helper prototypes take `void` rather than an unused `Fighter_GObj*`, and `ftKb_SpecialN_800F16D0` gained the `Fighter_GObj*` parameter reflected by callers.

Kirby Yoshi-copy special logic gained implementations for capture transition animation functions and `ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_Anim`, including grab timer reduction, mash handling, egg breakout effect spawn, iframe setup, and fall transition.

Popo/Ice Climbers side/up/down special work implemented or improved `ftPp_SpecialS_80120E68`, `ftPp_SpecialS_80120FE0`, `ftPp_SpecialHiStart_0_Anim`, `ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Anim`, `ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_0_Phys`, `ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow2_Phys`, `ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow2_Coll`, `ftPp_SpecialLw_Coll`, and `fn_80122D2C`.
Popo attributes in `types.h` were refined by replacing padding with named float fields `x7C`, `x9C`, `xA0`, `xB8`, `xBC`, and `xC0` used by the new special-move logic.
Nana collision functions gained small stack padding arrays, and `ftNn_Init_8012300C` was exposed in `ftNn_Init.h` for Popo's partner range/status check.
Ice Climbers item headers gained `itClimbersStringAttributes` and prototypes for `it_802C3864` and `it_802C3950`, enabling Popo string-item event dispatch.
smart_moves: Re-typed attribute offsets from integers to floats where the new code uses them in position, velocity, and random-placement math, which is both semantically clearer and matching-relevant.
Split Crazy Hand's previously flat selection table into `states`, `x48`, `x5C` range pairs, and `x68`, making the random AI state selection readable while preserving the original data layout.

Used small inline helper functions for repeated matching-sensitive sequences, such as Crazy Hand animation transitions, Popo callback setup, fall physics, Nana range checking, and Popo collision landing transitions.
Added explicit stack padding (`PAD_STACK`, `u8 _[...]`) in several functions where local layout mattered for matching, including Kirby Yoshi egg and Nana/Popo routines.
Used `#pragma dont_inline` around `ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_Anim`, indicating a targeted compiler-codegen control to keep the function matching.
Kept address-derived unknown names for not-yet-understood routines while still making type/prototype improvements, avoiding speculative semantic renames.
Connected fighter code to item headers instead of leaving item functions implicit, notably for Crazy Hand bombs, Kirby/Game & Watch chef items, and Ice Climbers string/blizzard articles.
decomp_lessons: When a decompiled routine consumes DAT attributes as positions, offsets, or multipliers, revisit the struct field types; changing `s32` padding/fields to `float` can unlock many downstream matches.
Anonymous byte blobs can often be decomposed into small typed helper structs without changing layout; this improves readability and makes random table indexing easier to reason about.
For fighter animation-state code, preserving exact motion-state arguments, callback assignment order, and command-variable resets is matching-sensitive.

Header prototypes should reflect whether helper functions truly consume a `Fighter_GObj*`; removing unused parameters in declarations and definitions can be necessary for matching and call-site cleanup.
Partner-character logic for Ice Climbers often needs explicit `Player_GetEntityAtIndex(player_id, 1)` checks and motion-id range checks before reading Nana joint positions.
Stack-shape hacks remain useful evidence-backed tactics: this PR used `PAD_STACK`, dummy `u8` arrays, and scoped locals to satisfy matching constraints.
Item article attributes may need small local structs in shared item headers so fighter code can dispatch article events without opaque pointer arithmetic.
naming_conventions: Unknown or partially understood functions retained project address-style names such as `ftCh_Init_80156AD8`, `fn_801588B8`, `ftPp_SpecialS_80120FE0`, and `fn_80122D2C`.
Motion-state constants use fighter prefixes such as `ftMh_MS_*`, `ftKb_MS_*`, and `ftPp_MS_*`.
DAT/ext-attribute locals commonly use short names like `da`, while fighter pointers use `fp`, `fp2`, `nana_fp`, and item pointers use `ip`.
Position-bearing struct fields gained `_pos` suffixes where already present, such as `x30_pos2`, `x88_pos`, `xA8_pos`, and `xBC_pos`; newly exposed unknown fields stayed offset-based.
Small local helper names are descriptive but scoped to the file, e.g. `checkNanaInRange`, `incrementMvAndCheck`, `doFallPhys`, `ftPp_set_cbs`, and `ftPp_SpecialLw_Coll_Land`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted `PAD_STACK(16)`, `PAD_STACK(12)`, `PAD_STACK(8)`, and dummy `u8 _[...]` locals to match stack frame layout.
Introduced custom `my_sqrtf` using `__frsqrte` refinement and volatile storage in Popo side-special velocity code, likely to match the original PPC sqrt sequence.
Used explicit inline wrappers for repeated codegen-sensitive chunks instead of only semantic refactoring.
Changed some conditionals to ternary expressions and adjusted literal types (`1.0f`, `0.0f`) where the diff indicates matching refinements.
Preserved odd control flow where necessary, including a labeled `end` block in `ftPp_SpecialS_80120FE0` with the note that original code returns without a value.
Added `u8_pair` and a typed Crazy Hand table to preserve exact global data layout while making index/range accesses clearer.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
Automated decomp.dev report: GALE01 matched code increased to 60.76% (+0.18%, +7136 bytes).

Automated decomp.dev report listed 20 new matches, including `ftCh_Wait1_0_Anim`, `fn_801588B8`, `fn_80122D2C`, `ftCh_Init_80156198`, `ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_Anim`, `ftKb_UnkIntBoolFunc0`, `ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow2_Phys`, and `fn_8015B548`.
Automated report also listed 8 improvements in unmatched items, including `ftCh_Init_80156AD8` reaching 98.86%, Popo start/collision routines improving, and two small `.sdata2` regressions.
follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching `ftCh_Init_80156AD8`, which was reported as 98.86% but not fully matched in this PR.
Search for subsequent cleanup of `ftPp_SpecialS_80120FE0` and its noted missing return-value behavior.
Look for later semantic renames of address-style helpers introduced here, especially `fn_801588B8`, `fn_8015B548`, and `fn_80122D2C`.
Check whether the Popo `.sdata2` and Crazy Hand `.sdata2` regressions from the automated report were resolved in follow-up work.
Search for further struct refinement of `ftIceClimberAttributes`, `ftCrazyHand_DatAttrs`, and `itClimbersStringAttributes`.
